title: Clock, Delta Time, Fixed Steps, Interpolation, and Stalls · Contract Workshop
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds bounded deterministic time-step policy from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded deterministic time-step policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.
Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.
Accumulate real time into bounded fixed simulation updates and preserve remainder for interpolation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Clock Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 4633 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Clock Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for call clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Clock Contract, keep Clock Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Time Evidence
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 8776 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Time Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-time-evidence-1dkafx" that uses case 4278 to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Time Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Time Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Time Evidence, keep Time Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-time-evidence-1dkafx
# Competency: asteroids-time-evidence.
# Case 4278: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_time_evidence_1dkafx(frame_seconds: float = 0.016, max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1, fixed_step: float = 1 / 120, accumulator: float = 0.0, max_updates: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1dkafx = "1dkafx-xfakd1-4278"
    assert evidence_variant_1dkafx
    evidence_axes_1dkafx = (
        "player1dkafx",
        "seed4278",
        "inputxfakd1",
        "frame1dkafx4278",
        "state42781dkafx",
        "playtestxfakd14278",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1dkafx)) == 6
    if frame_seconds < 0.0 or fixed_step <= 0.0:
        return False, frame_seconds, "time-domain"
    clamped = min(frame_seconds, max_frame_seconds)
    updates = int((accumulator + clamped) / fixed_step)
    if updates > max_updates:
        return False, updates, "spiral-guard"
    if clamped > max_frame_seconds:
        return False, clamped, "clamp-failure"
    assert 0 <= updates <= max_updates
    return True, updates, "bounded-time-step"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Time Evidence structural check is only one gate. bounded deterministic time-step policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
